title: 10 best herbal tinctures for anxiety

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with anxiety because of its unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that work synergistically to promote relaxation and calmness. The tincture's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stress, reducing feelings of overwhelm and promoting a sense of balance and tranquility. Additionally, maypop contains tryptophan, an amino acid that is converted into serotonin in the brain, which helps regulate mood and reduce anxiety symptoms. As a result, maypop tinctures can be a valuable natural tool for managing anxiety and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Maypop tincture, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm anxiety symptoms.
Alkaloids: Maypop contains alkaloids like harmine, which is known for its ability to bind to serotonin receptors, helping to regulate mood and reduce feelings of anxiety.
Saponins: The saponin content in Maypop tincture has been shown to have a tranquilizing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress by slowing down rapid heart rate and blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Hernandeziase: A flavonoid glycoside - Hernandeziase has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Isovitexin: A flavonoid - Isovitexin has been found to have sedative and anxiolytic effects by inhibiting the activity of glutamate, a neurotransmitter involved in anxiety and stress.

Harpagide: A steroidal glycoside - Harpagide has been reported to possess anxiolytic and sedative properties, possibly due to its ability to interact with GABA receptors and modulate the activity of neurotransmitters involved in anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their high concentration of flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to the plant's anxiolytic properties.
Fruits: The pulp and juice of the fruit are used, as they are rich in flavonoids, particularly vitexin, which is thought to be responsible for the plant's anxiety-reducing effects.
Barks: The bark of the plant is used due to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to have a synergistic effect in reducing anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves at peak potency when the plant is in full bloom and the flowers are fully open.
Clean and dry the harvested passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1 part passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every week to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains a unique set of compounds called kavalactones, which have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain. This interaction can help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety. Additionally, kava has been traditionally used for centuries in Pacific Island cultures to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of anxiety. By incorporating kava tinctures into your daily routine, you may find that you're better equipped to handle stressful situations and enjoy a greater sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kava's primary active compounds, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.

Anxiolytic effects: Kava's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties help alleviate feelings of unease, worry, and apprehension by modulating the body's response to stress, promoting relaxation and tranquility.

Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle-relaxing properties help calm muscle spasms, tension, and stiffness associated with anxiety, promoting a sense of physical relaxation and reducing overall stress levels.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, particularly kawain and methysticin, have anxiolytic and sedative effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, thereby reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Flavokawain B: Flavokawain B has potent anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate anxiety by modulating the body's stress response and reducing inflammation associated with anxiety disorders.

Myristicin: Myristicin, a terpene in Piper methysticum, has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects by inhibiting the activity of certain neurotransmitters that contribute to anxiety, such as serotonin and dopamine.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in kavalactones, which are the primary active compounds responsible for the anxiolytic and sedative effects of the plant.
Leaves: Contain a high concentration of kavalactones, making them a suitable choice for tinctures aimed at reducing anxiety.
Roots: Also contain kavalactones, which can be extracted to produce tinctures that help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest piper methysticum roots and leaves in early morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces and dry them at 35 degrees celsius for 7 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried piper methysticum with 1 liter of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with anxiety because it possesses a calming and soothing effect on the mind and body. The aromatic properties of lavender have been shown to reduce stress and promote relaxation, helping to calm an overactive nervous system. By taking a few drops of English lavender tincture, individuals can experience a sense of tranquility and peace, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep, as well as reducing symptoms of anxiety and panic attacks.
remedy_properties: Linalool: A naturally occurring compound found in lavender oil, linalool has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Lavender's GABA-boosting properties: Lavender contains compounds that increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and anxiety reduction.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender tinctures contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress through its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Linalyl acetate: This ester has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related muscle tension.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid exhibits anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce anxiety by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain high concentrations of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Leaves: They have a similar composition to flowers and are often used as a substitute in herbal remedies for anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 2 hours.
Chop 2 ounces of dried flowers into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped flowers with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and steep for 4 to 6 weeks in a dark glass jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store for 2 to 3 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to interact with the body's natural chemistry. The herb contains compounds that bind to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. This can help reduce feelings of worry and nervousness, allowing individuals to better cope with stressors in their daily lives. Additionally, valerian tinctures have been shown to promote deeper sleep, which is often disrupted by anxiety. By addressing both daytime and nighttime symptoms, valerian tinctures provide a comprehensive approach to managing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effects: Valerian root contains compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and calmness.
Adaptogenic properties: Valerian's adaptogenic qualities allow it to help the body adapt to stress and anxiety, reducing feelings of overwhelm and promoting a sense of balance and well-being.

GABA-enhancing effects: Valerian has been shown to increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and anxiety, leading to a decrease in anxiety symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has anxiolytic and sedative properties by inhibiting the activity of GABA receptors, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a short-chain fatty acid, has a calming effect on the nervous system by acting as an inhibitory neurotransmitter modulator, reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of well-being.
Valeranone: Valeranone, a valerenic acid derivative, possesses anxiolytic properties by interacting with the GABA receptor complex, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote a calm state.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound known for its calming effects.
Barks: The barks of Valeriana officinalis are used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their rich content of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis are used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their ability to absorb and concentrate the plant's active compounds, including valerenic acid.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 parts of dried valeriana officinalis roots and 1 part of dried aerial parts in a clean glass container.
Combine the gathered herbs with 70 proof vodka as a solvent at a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to small dark glass bottles for storage and use within 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with anxiety because of its natural adaptogenic properties, which enable it to balance the body's response to stress. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and calming the mind. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to increase levels of neurotransmitters like serotonin and GABA, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and reducing anxiety symptoms. As a result, using St John's Wort tinctures can lead to a sense of calm and reduced anxiety levels over time.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by inhibiting the reuptake of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in mood regulation.
Adhyperforin: Similar to hyperforin, adhyperforin also blocks the reuptake of serotonin and other neurotransmitters like dopamine and norepinephrine, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to St. John's Wort's anxiolytic properties by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that acts as a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI), which helps in increasing the levels of serotonin in the brain, thereby reducing anxiety and improving mood.

Hypericin: Hypericin is a naphthodianthrone that exhibits anxiolytic and antidepressant properties by interacting with the neurotransmitter systems, including serotonin and dopamine, to reduce anxiety and stress.

N-phenylethylamine (NEA): NEA is a phenethylamine alkaloid that has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by increasing the release of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which helps in reducing anxiety and stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are commonly used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are used to make tinctures for anxiety because they contain a high amount of hyperforin, a compound that has been demonstrated to have a positive effect on mood regulation and stress reduction.

Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum are also used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their rich content of alkaloids, such as hypericin, which has been found to possess anti-anxiety and antidepressant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers in sufficient quantities for a single batch of tincture.
Combine the hypericum perforatum flowers with a neutral solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1:2 to 1:5.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for four to six weeks stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material from the liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle and label it with the date and ingredients for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with anxiety because of its natural ability to calm the mind and body. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce stress levels and promote relaxation. When taken as a tincture, lemon balm's sweet, citrusy flavor can help soothe an anxious mind, calming symptoms such as racing thoughts and restlessness. Additionally, its sedative properties can aid in falling asleep faster and sleeping more soundly, making it an excellent natural remedy for those struggling with anxiety.
remedy_properties: GABA-Enhancing Properties: Lemon balm's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing anxiety.
Adrenal Fatigue Relief: The adaptogenic properties of lemon balm help to regulate the body's response to stress, alleviating adrenal fatigue and anxiety symptoms by balancing cortisol levels.

Anxiolytic Activity: Lemon balm has been found to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) activity through its ability to modulate neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinicine: This phenolic compound helps alleviate anxiety by exerting a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Melissic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, likely due to its ability to modulate neurotransmitters and reduce stress response.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has a sedative effect and helps reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation, reducing muscle tension, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of the essential oil and active compounds responsible for the calming effects.
Flowers: The flowers contain a higher concentration of melissic acid, which contributes to the anxiolytic properties of the plant.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis also contain essential oils and active compounds that contribute to the plant's calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of melissa officinalis flowers at peak bloom time typically in summer months for best potency.
Dry the flowers by spreading them out in a single layer in a warm dark place for 1 week.
Combine 1 part of dried melissa officinalis flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar every day to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime tinctures helps with anxiety because its unique blend of herbs, such as passionflower, lemon balm, and kava, work synergistically to calm the mind and body. The essential oils in these herbs have a sedative effect, reducing feelings of stress and nervousness, while also promoting relaxation and tranquility. This can lead to a significant reduction in anxiety symptoms, allowing individuals to feel more centered and grounded. By using herbal lime tinctures, people can find relief from anxiety without relying on pharmaceuticals or other harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal lime tinctures contain adaptogens like ashwagandha, ginseng, and rhodiola rosea, which help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of balance and calmness.
Amino acid content: These tinctures are rich in amino acids like L-theanine, which has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety by increasing GABA production in the brain.

Essential oils and terpenes: The citrus extracts in herbal lime tinctures, such as limonene and pinene, have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety symptoms like feelings of restlessness and irritability.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit anti-anxiety effects by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing inflammation in the brain.

Terpenoids (specifically, alpha-pinene and borneol): These terpenoids contribute to the anxiolytic properties of Tilia europaea by interacting with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Phenolic acids (specifically, rosmarinic acid): This phenolic acid helps alleviate anxiety by acting as a antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which in turn reduces oxidative stress and promotes a sense of calm.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their calming effects, as they contain compounds like tiliroside and ursolic acid, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote sleep.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their sedative properties, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves and flowers of tilia europaea on a dry day in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Grind 1 part of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part of the powdered plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to calm the mind and body. The extract from this tree contains flavonoids and terpenes that work together to reduce stress and promote relaxation. By soothing the nervous system, Maidenhair tree tincture can alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness, irritability, and difficulty concentrating. Additionally, its gentle sedative properties help quiet the mind, allowing for a sense of calm and tranquility to wash over the body. This natural remedy can provide a peaceful escape from the chaos of everyday life, promoting overall well-being and balance.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Maidenhair tree tincture has adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in response to anxiety-inducing situations. This property can help reduce anxiety symptoms by normalizing cortisol levels and improving mental resilience.

Anxiolytic activity: The tincture contains flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate feelings of anxiety, worry, and fear.

GABA-mimetic action: Maidenhair tree tincture has been found to mimic the actions of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation responses. This property can help calm anxious thoughts and promote feelings of serenity.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, help reduce anxiety by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of neurotransmitters that regulate mood.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anxiolytic effects by modulating the activity of GABA (inhibitory neurotransmitter) receptors and reducing the release of stress hormones like cortisol.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, a group of diterpene trilactones, help alleviate anxiety by blocking the binding of serotonin to its receptors, which can reduce feelings of anxiety and stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Seeds: Contain ginkgotoxine, a compound that may help alleviate anxiety by modulating neurotransmitter activity.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by regulating stress hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Dry the leaves in a warm place at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 to 4 weeks.
Chop 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Mix the chopped leaves with 80 percent vodka and 20 percent glycerin in a 1:3 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place then strain and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain natural compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system. The tincture is made by infusing oats in a solvent, which extracts their beneficial properties, such as beta-glucans and flavonoids. These compounds help to reduce stress hormones like cortisol, promote relaxation, and soothe an overactive mind. Additionally, herbal oats tinctures have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a sense of calm and reduced anxiety.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid, have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a sense of calm and relaxation.

Silica: Silica, a type of soluble fiber found in oats, has been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress-induced inflammation and promote relaxation, contributing to its anxiolytic effects.

Beta-glucans: Beta-glucans, complex carbohydrates found in oats, have been shown to modulate the immune response and reduce cortisol levels, leading to a decrease in anxiety-related symptoms such as increased heart rate and blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These unique polyphenolic compounds have been shown to have anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids like avenacoside and avenalol may contribute to the anxiolytic properties of Avena sativa by regulating the body's stress response and promoting a sense of calm.

Furofuran lignans: These lignans, including avenasterol and avenasterol acetate, may help alleviate anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and influencing the brain's stress response, promoting a state of relaxation and reduced anxiety.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to their anxiolytic and calming effects.
Leaves: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Roots: Used for their sedative properties, as they contain a range of bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh avena sativa roots and 1 pound of fresh avena sativa leaves in the morning.
Chop the avena sativa roots into small pieces and combine with the chopped avena sativa leaves in a container.
Soak the chopped avena sativa mixture in 2 cups of 80 proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid from the avena sativa mixture through cheesecloth into a container and discard the solids.
Bottle the herbal avena sativa tincture in small glass bottles and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have been shown to promote relaxation and calmness. The inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in chicory root, has been linked to reduced symptoms of anxiety by promoting a healthy gut microbiome. Additionally, the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in chicory leaves and roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate feelings of stress and unease, leading to a sense of tranquility and reduced anxiety levels.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Chicory tincture contains saponins, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by slowing down the heart rate and blood pressure.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan found in chicory, has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, possibly due to its ability to modulate the gut-brain axis and promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.

Flavonoids: Chicory tincture is rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce stress and anxiety by protecting the brain from oxidative damage and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, helps with anxiety by promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation due to its prebiotic properties, which support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
Cichorin: Cichorin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by modulating the activity of GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a reduction in anxiety symptoms.

Chicoric acid: Chicoric acid, a caffeoylquinic acid derivative, exhibits anxiolytic and anti-stress properties by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the production of stress hormones, such as cortisol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cichorium intybus are used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for anxiety because they contain a high concentration of inulin and sesquiterpenes, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Flowers: The flowers of Cichorium intybus are used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their rich content of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have a sedative effect and can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried cichorium intybus leaves and flowers and combine with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate clean glass container discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to small amber glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to mature.
Label the bottles with the date and contents and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains compounds that interact with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation. The licorice root extract has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of stress and apprehension. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate physical symptoms of anxiety such as tension headaches and muscle strain, allowing individuals to better manage their emotional responses and find peace.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an adaptogen, helping the body adapt to stress and reducing feelings of anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-anxiety and sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of anxiety, such as rapid heartbeat and muscle tension.

Isoliquiritigenin: Has been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by increasing the expression of GABA receptors in the brain, which helps to promote relaxation and reduce feelings of anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic properties and can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and saponins that have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra root 200 grams in bulk or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Chop the dried root into small pieces and transfer it to a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine 80 proof vodka or ethanol 500 milliliters with the chopped root and seal the jar tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks shaking daily to ensure efficient extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work to calm the mind and body. The alpha acids present in hops, specifically humulone and lupulone, have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress and anxiety. Additionally, the bitter flavor of hops has also been known to stimulate the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as stomach butterflies and digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Hops contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxious thoughts and promote relaxation.
Anxiolytic properties: The alpha acids in hops have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress.

Adaptogenic properties: Hops contain compounds that are known for their adaptogenic effects, meaning they help the body adapt to stressors and promote balance in the body, which can be particularly helpful for individuals experiencing chronic anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hops roots have a sedative and calming effect, making them ideal for soothing anxiety symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the hops plant contain flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties and help reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hops cones from a trusted source or purchase dried hops for the most effective tincture.
Cleanly chop 200g of dried hops into small pieces and combine with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to release the active compounds from the hops.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing gently to extract every last drop.
Store the finished tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, using 20-30 drops per serving as needed for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The herb's natural compounds, including citral and limonene, work together to reduce feelings of stress and overwhelm, allowing individuals to feel more centered and grounded. By soothing the mind and body, lemon verbena tinctures provide a sense of tranquility, making it easier to manage anxiety and find peace in the midst of chaos.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic activity: Lemon verbena's flavonoids, particularly nuciferin and vitexin, have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin.
Sedative properties: The tincture's sesquiterpenes, such as limonene and geranyl acetate, can help calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena's high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to anxiety disorders by reducing inflammation and stress-induced damage in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon verbena plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties and help calm nervous tension.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation by regulating the body's stress response.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality aloysia citriodora leaves and stems for their calming effects.
Combine the harvested plant material with 80% vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The menthol in peppermint oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body, while also improving focus and concentration. Additionally, peppermint's aromatic compounds can help to uplift and refresh the spirit, reducing feelings of overwhelm and anxiety, allowing for a sense of calm and clarity.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Peppermint's ability to calm digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), can help reduce anxiety by alleviating physical discomfort and promoting overall well-being.

Mood-Stabilizing Flavonoids: Peppermint contains flavonoids like rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and jitteriness.

Cognitive Enhancing Compounds: Peppermint's menthol content can increase alertness and focus by stimulating the brain's neurotransmitters, helping to counterbalance anxiety-induced fogginess and mental fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of anxiety.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint rhyzomes have a rich source of menthol, which has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties and can help to soothe anxious thoughts and feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh peppermint leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 200g of the cleaned peppermint leaves with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the resulting peppermint tincture in dark-tinted amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Five flavor berry tinctures helps with anxiety because it combines the calming properties of ashwagandha, passionflower, and lemon balm to soothe an overactive mind. The adaptogenic herbs in the blend help regulate stress hormones, while the sweet and tangy flavors of blueberry and hawthorn berries provide a sense of calm and relaxation. As the tincture is absorbed into the body, it promotes a peaceful state, reducing feelings of worry and unease, allowing individuals to better cope with everyday anxiety-inducing situations.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Helps to reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress, promoting balance and resilience.
Amino acid content: Rich in amino acids such as GABA, 5-HTP, and L-theanine, which are converted into neurotransmitters that calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Polyphenol antioxidant content: High levels of antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to anxiety and other mood disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the five flavor berry plant are rich in calming compounds that help alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain bioactive compounds that have a sedative effect, making them an effective component for anxiety tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried schisandra chinensis berries in 10-15% ethanol solution and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine 20 grams of the dried schisandra chinensis berries with 100 ml of 80% proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and infusion of the herb's active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and setting the liquid aside for further processing.

Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing them in the refrigerator at 40°f to 50°f (4°c to 10°c) and allowing it to mature for a minimum of 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain the natural herb, Vitex agnus-castus, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. The tincture's active compounds, such as iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce stress hormones like cortisol, promoting feelings of relaxation and reducing symptoms of anxiety. Additionally, chaste tree has been traditionally used to support hormonal balance, which can also contribute to reduced anxiety levels. By taking a chaste tree tincture, individuals may experience a sense of calm and clarity, helping them manage their anxiety and live a more balanced life.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating effects: Chaste tree tincture helps regulate estrogen levels, which can alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress by promoting a sense of calm and balance.
Adrenal support: The tincture's adaptogenic properties help support the adrenal glands, reducing feelings of anxiety and fatigue by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.
GABA-enhancing effects: Chaste tree contains flavonoids that have been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) activity in the brain, helping to calm anxiety and promote a sense of serenity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, roots help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms.
Seeds: High in vitamins and minerals, seeds provide a gentle yet effective way to soothe anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus-castus leaves stems flowers and roots in sufficient quantity.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part of chopped plant material with five parts of pure vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for slow percolation and extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for anxiety are liquid extracts of herbs used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, such as restlessness and worry. These tinctures work by interacting with the brain's chemistry to promote relaxation and calmness. The best herbal tinctures for anxiety include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Humulus lupulus, Melissa officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, anxiety can lead to sleep disturbances, decreased productivity, strained relationships, and even physical health problems, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to anxiety that are treated with herbal tinctures are stress, insomnia, and depression. Tinctures made from herbs such as Ashwagandha, Kava, and Valerian root have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, promote relaxation, and improve mood. Other conditions like restlessness, irritability, and fatigue can also be alleviated with herbal tinctures, offering a natural alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with anxiety is a blend of ashwagandha, passionflower, and lavender. Ashwagandha is known for its adaptogenic properties, helping the body adapt to stress. Passionflower calms the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Lavender, with its soothing scent, further calms the mind and body. Combining these tinctures can provide a holistic approach to managing anxiety, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. A typical ratio is 20-30% ashwagandha, 30-40% passionflower, and 20-30% lavender.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Pharmacological research, herbal tinctures are good for anxiety because certain medicinal herbs have been found to be effective in reducing anxiety symptoms. The study, which conducted a Bayesian network meta-analysis of 29 trials comparing 12 medicinal herbs, found that Silexan displayed a significant effect on anxiety, with a mean difference of -3.84 in endpoint Hamilton Anxiety Scale (HAMA) scores. Additionally, other herbs such as Kava, Ginkgo biloba, and Withania somnifera were also found to be efficacious in reducing anxiety symptoms, although some of these findings were based on limited sample sizes and further study is needed to confirm their effectiveness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures helps with anxiety because of its ability to interact with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as valerenic acid and valeranone, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, calming the mind and body. This natural anxiolytic properties make Valeriana officinalis a popular herbal remedy for anxiety relief, promoting a sense of calmness and tranquility, and helping individuals manage anxiety symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid acts as a GABA receptor modulator, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Valeranone: Valeranone has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by inhibiting the activity of neurotransmitters that contribute to anxiety, such as serotonin and norepinephrine.
Valerenol: Valerenol exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties by enhancing the activity of GABA, a neurotransmitter that helps to regulate the activity of nerve cells and reduce anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound known for its anxiolytic properties.
Roots: The root's valerenic acid content is responsible for its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, making it a preferred choice for tinctures.
Roots: The root's bioactive compounds, including valepotriates and isovalerenic acid, contribute to its anxiolytic and calming effects, making it a popular choice for anxiety tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to remove dirt and moisture using a clean towel or paper.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase their surface area.
Steep one part of dried roots in two parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that have a calming effect on the nervous system. The tincture works by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, which helps to regulate mood and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Passiflora incarnata tinctures can help individuals cope with anxiety-related conditions, leading to improved overall well-being and a better quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Isopasyloflavone: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, increasing the activity of the inhibitory neurotransmitter GABA, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Flavonoids: Possesses anxiolytic and sedative properties by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and influencing the brain's response to stress, leading to a reduction in anxiety symptoms.
Alkaloids (specifically, harmine and harmaline): Exhibits anxiolytic and sedative effects by interacting with the brain's serotonin and dopamine systems, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are responsible for the plant's anxiolytic properties.
Fruits: They are a rich source of serotonin and other neurotransmitter-boosting compounds that can help alleviate anxiety.
Barks: They contain a significant amount of passiflorine, a key alkaloid responsible for the plant's anxiolytic and sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Combine the dried flowers and leaves with 60 percent ethanol or vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a sealed glass jar with occasional agitation and a temperature range of 60-80 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties. The bioactive compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. This can lead to a decrease in anxiety symptoms and an improvement in overall mood. By supporting the body's natural response to stress, lavender tinctures can help individuals feel more calm and centered, making it a popular natural remedy for anxiety relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with anxiety by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress responses.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to anxiety relief by modulating the brain's neurotransmitter balance, specifically enhancing the effects of GABA and reducing excitatory neurotransmitters.
Perillyl alcohol: Exhibits anxiolytic properties by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the breakdown of GABA, ultimately leading to increased GABA levels and reduced anxiety.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain high levels of linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and sedative properties.
Leaves: They are also rich in linalool and other essential oils that contribute to their anxiolytic and relaxing effects.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain a mix of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and prepare a clean glass jar for storage.
Measure out 1 part dried flowers by weight and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 parts high-proof vodka to the jar and seal it tightly for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains adaptogenic compounds that promote balance and relaxation in the body. The tincture is derived from the oat plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe the nervous system. By stimulating the parasympathetic nervous system, Avena sativa tinctures can help calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety. This natural remedy can also improve sleep quality and enhance mood, leading to a greater sense of well-being and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Lignans help with anxiety by interacting with neurotransmitters to promote relaxation and reduce stress response.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly avenanthramides, help with anxiety by exerting a sedative effect and modulating the body's stress response.
Saponins: Saponins help with anxiety by interacting with neurotransmitters to promote a calming effect and reduce anxiety-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anxiolytic and sedative properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Seeds: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Leaves: They are used for their adaptogenic and calming effects, which can help regulate stress response and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of avena sativa roots in the autumn season when they are mature and dry.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with a gentle stream of water to remove dirt.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 2 parts of vodka in a jar.
Steep the chopped roots in the vodka mixture for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. The tinctures promote relaxation by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of restlessness and irritability. Additionally, ginger's natural ability to regulate digestion and alleviate nausea can help ease feelings of overwhelm and anxiety, promoting a sense of balance and calmness in the body. This leads to a decrease in anxiety symptoms and an improvement in overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol helps with anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms.

Gingerols (6-Gingerol and 8-Gingerol): These sesquiterpene compounds help with anxiety by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and anxiety.

Shogaols (6-Shogaol and 8-Shogaol): These ginger-derived compounds help with anxiety by interacting with the body's opioid receptors, reducing pain perception, and promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high content of gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties.
Roots: Roots are often used as they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Stems: Stems are occasionally used, although to a lesser extent than rhyzomes and roots, due to their lower content of bioactive compounds, but still contribute to the overall anxiolytic effects of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots of zingiber officinale in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces weighing about 1/2 pound or 250 grams.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms. Ginkgo biloba also enhances blood flow to the brain, promoting better communication between neurons and potentially reducing symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness and irritability. Additionally, it has been shown to have a balancing effect on neurotransmitters, supporting emotional well-being and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have a neuroprotective effect, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
Bilobalide: It has a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Ginkgolides: They have a modulating effect on neurotransmitters, particularly serotonin and dopamine, which can help regulate mood and reduce anxiety symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are most commonly used to make tinctures for anxiety due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anxiolytic effects.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are used in tinctures for anxiety as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also used in tinctures for anxiety, as they contain ginkgolic acids, which may help reduce anxiety by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Dry the leaves thoroughly in a single layer at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit for 6-8 hours.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:2 leaves to vodka by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar at room temperature for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains a high concentration of hops' active compounds, including alpha-acid and beta-acid. These compounds interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The tinctures' sedative properties also help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, humulus lupulus tinctures have been traditionally used to soothe nervous tension and promote a sense of calm, making it a natural remedy for anxiety relief.
remedy_constituents: Lupulones: These bitter compounds help with anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation through their calming effects on the nervous system.
Humulone: As a precursor to lupulones, humulone contributes to the anxiolytic properties of Humulus lupulus by reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calm.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-anxiety effects by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps alleviate anxiety symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Humulus lupulus to make tinctures for anxiety due to their high content of essential oils, including humulone, which has anxiolytic properties.
Stems: The stems, or more specifically, the hop cones which grow from the stems, contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help alleviate anxiety.
Leaves: The leaves of Humulus lupulus may also be used in tinctures for anxiety, as they are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh or dried humulus lupulus flowers and leaves on a dry day to ensure quality.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Fill a glass jar with 80-proof vodka or glycerin covering the chopped plant material by at least an inch.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow the tincture to mature and extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures helps with anxiety because they contain high levels of melissic acid and linalool, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. The herb has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. Its anxiolytic properties help to slow down rapid breathing and heart rate, leading to a sense of calm and tranquility. By reducing anxiety and stress, Melissa officinalis tinctures can improve sleep quality and overall mood, making it a popular natural remedy for anxiety relief.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Helps with anxiety by acting as a natural anti-anxiety agent, reducing stress hormones and promoting relaxation.
Luteolin: Helps with anxiety by modulating neurotransmitters and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety and stress.
Linalool: Helps with anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related symptoms through its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of the herb's medicinal compounds, including rosmarinic acid, which contributes to its anxiolytic properties.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and citral, which are known for their calming and sedative effects.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis contain a combination of flavonoids and terpenoids, which work together to provide a sense of relaxation and reduced anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the melissa flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried melissa flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with anxiety because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, compounds that interact with neurotransmitters in the brain. These interactions can increase serotonin and dopamine levels, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a natural adaptogen, the tincture also helps the body adapt to stress, reducing feelings of overwhelm and anxiety. Additionally, its sedative properties can soothe the nervous system, leading to a more balanced emotional state and improved mood regulation.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has a potent anxiolytic effect by interacting with the brain's serotonin system, thereby reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Emodin: As a flavonoid, emodin has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps alleviate symptoms of anxiety by reducing stress and promoting a sense of well-being.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its anxiolytic effects by reducing oxidative stress and promoting a balanced mood.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which are responsible for the plant's antidepressant and anxiolytic properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their content of hypericin and hyperforin, which helps in reducing anxiety and stress.
Stems: They are used due to their alkaloid content, which contributes to the plant's anxiolytic and antidepressant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh st johns wort flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of 25% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with anxiety because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to stress. The tincture contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate anxiety-related physical symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can provide relief from anxiety and promote overall well-being. Regular use may also improve sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties, which help reduce stress and anxiety by inhibiting the release of stress hormones like cortisol.
Licopenoids (Glycosides): These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine.
Terpenoids (Saponins): These natural compounds have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, reducing anxiety by modulating the activity of the GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a calming effect.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-anxiety and stress-relieving properties.
Leaves: They are used to create herbal tinctures that can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation due to their adaptogenic properties.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make tinctures, which can help reduce anxiety by promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried roots from mature plants in late summer or early fall.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly.
